title: Process sulfonation of aminoethylene sulfonic ester to produce taurine
abstract: A process comprises continuously adding a first stream and a second stream to a sulfonation vessel, wherein the first stream comprises aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) and the second stream comprises an aqueous solution of sodium sulfite (Na2SO3). The process comprises continuously mixing the AES and the aqueous solution of Na2SO3 in the sulfonation vessel, thus producing a mixture. The process comprises continuously subjecting the mixture to heat in the presence of an inert gas, thus converting the AES to the taurine via sulfonation. In an aspect, the AES has a residence time of no more than four hours in the sulfonation vessel. In an aspect the heating step is conducted at a temperature of at least 115Â° C and a pressure of at least 200 psi.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A process for producing taurine, comprising: a) continuously adding a first stream and a second stream to a sulfonation vessel, wherein the first stream comprises aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) and the second stream comprises an aqueous solution of sodium sulfite (Na2SC>3); b) continuously mixing the aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) and the aqueous solution of sodium sulfite (Na2SC>3) in the sulfonation vessel, thus producing a mixture; and c) continuously subjecting the mixture to heat in the presence of an inert gas, thus converting the aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) to the taurine via sulfonation. 2. The process of claim 1, wherein the aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) has a residence time of no more than four hours in the sulfonation vessel. 3. The process of claim 1, wherein the aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) has a residence time of no more than two hours in the sulfonation vessel. 4. The process of claim 1, wherein the inert gas is selected from the group consisting of nitrogen, argon, helium, and combinations thereof. 5. The process of claim 1, wherein the inert gas is nitrogen. 6. The process of claim 1, further comprising subjecting the mixture to a pressure greater than autogenous pressure. 7. The process of claim 1, wherein the presence of the inert gas subjects the mixture to the pressure greater than autogenous pressure. 8. The process of claim 1, further comprising subjecting the mixture to a pressure to at least 50 psi. 9. The process of claim 1, further comprising subjecting the mixture to a pressure of at least 100 psi. 10. The process of claim 1, further comprising subjecting the mixture to a pressure of at least 200 psi. 11. The process of claim 1, wherein the heat is a temperature of at least 115Â°C. 12. The process of claim 1, wherein the heat is a temperature of at least 120Â° C. 13. The process of claim 1, wherein the heat is a temperature of 120-155Â° C. 14. The process of claim 1, wherein the heat is a temperature of 140-155Â° C. 15. The process of claim 1, wherein aminoethanol sulfate ester is continuously synthesized upstream of the vessel. 16. The process of claim 1, wherein aminoethanol sulfate ester is continuously synthesized upstream of the vessel by reacting monoethanolamine and sulfuric acid. 17. The process of claim 16, wherein aminoethanol sulfate ester is continuously dried upstream of the vessel after formed by reacting monoethanolamine and sulfuric acid. 18. The process of claim 17, wherein aminoethanol sulfate ester is continuously dried by at least one of a spray dryer or thin film evaporator. 19. The process of claim 1, further comprising separating taurine from Na 2 S0 4 and Na 2 S0 . 20. The process of claim 19, wherein the separating taurine from Na 2 S0 4 and Na 2 S0 3 is performed by chromatography. 21. The process of claim 19, wherein taurine is crystallized after separating taurine from Na 2 S04 and Na 2 S0 3 . 22. The process of claim 21, wherein crystallized taurine is separated from liquid after crystallization. 23. The process of claim 22, wherein the liquid separated from the crystallized taurine comprises unreacted monoethanolamine (MEA) and ami noethanol sulfate ester (AES). 24. The process of claim 23, wherein unreacted monoethanolamine (MEA) and aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) is recycled to a reactor wherein ami noethanol sulfate ester (AES) is synthesized upstream of the sulfonation vessel. 25. The process of claim 1, further comprising cooling the taurine, thereby crystallizing the taurine. 26. The process of claim 25, wherein the cooling is at a temperature of about 28Â° C. 27. The process of claim 1, wherein the vessel is an upflow sulfonation reactor. 28. The process of claim 1, wherein the vessel is a downflow sulfonation reactor. 29. The process of claim 1, wherein the first stream comprising aminoethanol sulfate ester (AES) and the second stream comprising an aqueous solution of sodium sulfite (Na2SC>3) are each degassed with an inert gas prior to being continuously added to the sulfonation vessel. 30. The process of claim 30, wherein the inert gas is selected from the group consisting of nitrogen, argon, helium, and combinations thereof. 31. The process of claim 30, wherein the inert gas is nitrogen.
